title: Exceptional Riemann-Roch Theorems
speaker: Chris Eur
abstract: For a smooth projective variety, the classical Hirzebruch--Riemann--Roch (HRR) theorem asserts the isomorphism between the Chow ring and the Grothendieck K-ring of vector bundles over rational coefficients. For certain toric varieties, we show an integral isomorphism between the two rings, different from the classical HRR, and discuss its utility in combinatorial contexts. Joint works with Andrew Berget, Alex Fink, June Huh, Hunter Spink, Dennis Tseng.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Motivation from K- and Chow-theoretic combinatorics
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates the need to relate K-rings and Chow rings by explaining how combinatorial objects such as matroids and delta-matroids can produce vector-bundle/K-classes and Chern/Chow classes whose numerical invariants encode combinatorics.

proof_stages.label: Toric combinatorial models for K(sigma) and A(sigma)
proof_stages.summary: For a projective unimodular fan, deformations give indicator functions generating a combinatorial K-ring and support functions generating a Chow ring of piecewise polynomials modulo global linear functions, with Euler characteristic and degree realized as lattice-point counts and normalized volumes.

proof_stages.label: Type-A permutohedral specialization and Postnikov formulas
proof_stages.summary: Specializing to the permutohedral fan, generalized permutohedra admit unique signed Minkowski decompositions into standard simplices, and Postnikov's theorem expresses their volumes by a dragon-marriage matching condition and their lattice-point counts by a transform of the volume polynomial.

proof_stages.label: Classical Riemann-Roch as an insufficient bridge
proof_stages.summary: The usual rational Chern-character/exponential isomorphism from K to Chow only makes Euler characteristic agree with degree after multiplication by the Todd class, whose Bernoulli-number complexity does not transparently explain the combinatorial lattice-point formulas.

proof_stages.label: Integral exceptional Riemann-Roch for the type-A permutohedral fan
proof_stages.summary: The main type-A theorem gives a unique integral ring isomorphism zeta from K to Chow sending standard-simplex indicator classes to simple geometric-series expressions in support functions, together with a Riemann-Roch formula replacing the Todd class by a simple permutohedral factor and thereby recovering Postnikov and matroid identities.

proof_stages.label: Proof mechanisms for the exceptional isomorphism
proof_stages.summary: The original proof compares explicit T-equivariant descriptions by piecewise Laurent polynomials and piecewise polynomials, while a later geometric proof views the phenomenon through the projective-space case and the distinguished embedding of the permutohedral variety into products of projective spaces.

proof_stages.label: Type-B signed permutohedral setup and exceptional isomorphism
proof_stages.summary: The type-B analogue is set up using the normal fan of the signed-permutation polytope, signed subsets, and origin-containing signed simplices, leading to a unique integral K-to-Chow isomorphism with an analogous simple Riemann-Roch correction factor.

proof_stages.label: Type-B Postnikov analogues and open extensions
proof_stages.summary: The type-B theorem yields a mirror of Postnikov's results: unique signed-simplex decompositions, volume formulas via signed transversals and matching, and lattice-point counts from a transformed volume polynomial, followed by questions about other Coxeter types, affine type A, type C, Weyl actions, and computational applications.

examples.name: Projective space as the model for the geometric proof
examples.note: The familiar case of projective space, where K and Chow are both polynomial quotients and a hyperplane class gives the desired type of map, is presented as the observation suggesting the more geometric proof via embeddings into products of projective spaces.
examples.engagement_reason: Projective space is analyzed as the model case where both rings are polynomial quotients and the relevant class map is described explicitly.
